sanskrit:
अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम्।
मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते॥
transliteration: anubandhaṃ kṣayaṃ hiṃsām anapekṣya ca pauruṣam | mohād ārabhyate karma yat tat tāmasam ucyate ||
literal_meaning: That action which is undertaken from delusion, disregarding consequence, loss, harm, and capacity, is declared to be of the mode of tamas (ignorance).
contextual_meaning: Actions driven by unconscious fog ignore long-term costs, harm others, and over-estimate personal power; they are psychic quicksand that deepens bondage.
psychological_state: Acting under the spell of tamas: narrowed attention, blunted empathy, denial of future impact, and compulsive short-term relief-seeking.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media at 2 a.m., sending a rage-tweet that ruins a career, then feeling blank surprise at the backlash.
anchor_text: Pause before acting; check if fog hides harm, cost, or truth.
